name: Chicken Parmesan with Spaghetti
steps: Cook spaghetti per package directions.
Dip chicken in egg, then breadcrumbs. Pan-sear 3-4 min per side until golden.
Top with pasta sauce and mozzarella, broil 2-3 min until melted.
Serve over spaghetti with parmesan.
steps_detailed: Bring a pot of salted water to a boil for the spaghetti.
Set up a dredging station: flour in one shallow dish, beaten egg in a second, and panko mixed with parmesan and oregano in a third.
Season the pounded chicken with salt and pepper. Dredge in flour (shake off excess), dip in egg, then press firmly into the panko mixture so it adheres in an even crust.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat. Cook the chicken 3-4 minutes per side until deep golden and cooked through — an instant-read thermometer should read 165F at the thickest point.
Meanwhile, cook the spaghetti per package directions, reserving a splash of pasta water before draining.
Spoon pasta sauce over the chicken, top with mozzarella, and either broil for 2 minutes until melted and bubbling, or cover the pan and let residual heat melt it.
Toss the drained spaghetti with a little extra sauce and the reserved pasta water if needed to loosen it. Serve alongside the chicken, topped with torn basil.

name: Caprese Stuffed Chicken
steps: Cook quinoa per package directions.
Stuff chicken with mozzarella, tomato, and basil; secure with toothpicks.
Bake at 400F for 20-25 min until cooked through.
Drizzle with balsamic and serve over quinoa.
steps_detailed: Simmer the balsamic vinegar and honey in a small saucepan over low heat for 5-7 minutes until reduced by about half and syrupy. Set aside.
Butterfly the chicken breast (slice horizontally, not quite all the way through) and open it like a book. Season the inside with salt, pepper, and the minced garlic.
Layer the mozzarella, tomato slices, and basil leaves inside, then fold the chicken closed and secure with toothpicks if needed.
Heat olive oil in an oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat. Sear the chicken 3 minutes per side until golden.
Transfer to a 375F oven and bake 12-15 minutes until cooked through (165F internal temperature).
Meanwhile, cook the quinoa per package directions.
Slice the chicken, drizzle with the balsamic reduction, and serve over the quinoa.

name: Shrimp Scampi
steps: Cook spaghetti per package directions.
Saute garlic in olive oil 30 sec, add shrimp and cook 2-3 min per side.
Toss shrimp with pasta, lemon juice, and parsley.
steps_detailed: Bring a pot of salted water to a boil and cook the spaghetti per package directions, reserving 1/4 cup pasta water before draining.
Pat the shrimp dry — this matters more than it seems, since wet shrimp will steam instead of sear.

Heat olive oil and half the butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the shrimp in a single layer and cook 1-2 minutes per side until just pink; don't overcook, they'll finish in the sauce. Remove to a plate.
Lower the heat to medium, add the garlic and red pepper flakes, and cook 30 seconds until fragrant but not browned.
Pour in the wine, scraping up any browned bits, and simmer 2 minutes to cook off the alcohol and reduce slightly.
Stir in the lemon juice and remaining butter until the sauce comes together and looks glossy.
Return the shrimp to the pan along with the drained spaghetti and a splash of the reserved pasta water. Toss to coat everything in the sauce.
Finish with parsley and a scattering of parmesan.

name: Turkey Bolognese
steps: Cook spaghetti per package directions.
Brown turkey, breaking it up, about 6-8 min.
Add pasta sauce, simmer 10 min. Serve over spaghetti with parmesan.
steps_detailed: Heat olive oil in a heavy pot over medium heat. Add the onion, carrot, and celery (the soffritto — the flavor base of the sauce) and cook 6-8 minutes, stirring occasionally, until softened but not browned.
Add the garlic and cook 30 seconds until fragrant.
Push the vegetables to the side, add the turkey to the cleared space, and brown it, breaking it apart with a spoon, about 5-6 minutes.
Stir in the tomato paste and cook 1-2 minutes — this deepens its flavor before the liquid goes in.
Pour in the wine and let it simmer 2-3 minutes to cook off the harsh alcohol edge.
Add the pasta sauce, milk, oregano, and bay leaf. The milk is a classic bolognese touch — it mellows the acidity of the tomatoes.
Reduce heat to low and simmer uncovered for 20-25 minutes, stirring occasionally, until thickened. Remove the bay leaf before serving.
Cook the spaghetti per package directions and toss with the sauce, or serve the sauce spooned over. Top with parmesan.

name: Italian Chicken & White Bean Jar
steps: Layer dressing at the bottom of a jar, then beans, tomato, chicken, and basil.
Shake to combine before eating.
steps_detailed: Whisk the balsamic vinegar, olive oil, and garlic together for the dressing.
In a bowl, combine the beans, tomatoes, red onion, and basil. Toss with about half the dressing.
Layer the dressed bean mixture in the bottom of a jar, top with the chicken, and drizzle the remaining dressing over top before sealing.

name: Baked Ziti with Turkey
steps: Cook pasta per package directions.
Brown turkey, add pasta sauce, combine with pasta.
Top with mozzarella, bake at 375F for 10 min until melted.
steps_detailed: Preheat the oven to 375F and bring a pot of salted water to a boil for the pasta.
Brown the turkey in a skillet over medium-high heat with the garlic, breaking it into small pieces, about 5-6 minutes.
Stir in the pasta sauce and dried basil, simmer 5 minutes.
Cook the pasta 2 minutes short of package directions — it'll finish cooking in the oven, so slightly underdone now prevents mush later.
In a small bowl, mix the ricotta with the egg — this is what gives baked ziti its creamy interior layer rather than dry, separated cheese.
In a baking dish, combine the pasta with the turkey sauce. Dollop the ricotta mixture over top and swirl it partway in with a knife.
Scatter mozzarella and parmesan over everything.
Bake uncovered for 18-20 minutes until bubbling and golden on top. Rest 5 minutes before serving, topped with fresh basil.

name: Chicken Cacciatore
steps: Sear chicken until browned.
Add pepper, onion, and tomatoes, simmer covered 25 min until cooked through.
steps_detailed: Pat the chicken dry and season generously with salt and pepper.

Heat olive oil in a heavy pot or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Sear the chicken skin-side down (if skin-on) until deeply golden, 4-5 minutes, then flip and sear the other side 3 minutes. Remove to a plate — it won't be fully cooked yet, that happens later.

Lower the heat to medium. Add the onion, both bell peppers, carrot, and celery to the same pot (the fond left from searing the chicken is flavor, don't wipe it out). Cook 6-8 minutes until the vegetables start to soften.
Add the garlic and cook 30 seconds until fragrant.
Stir in the tomato paste and cook 1 minute to deepen its flavor, then add the diced tomatoes, wine, broth, oregano, rosemary, bay leaf, and red pepper flakes.
Nestle the chicken back into the pot, spooning some sauce over the top. Bring to a simmer.
Cover and simmer on low for 30-35 minutes, until the chicken is fork-tender and fully cooked (165F). Uncover for the last 10 minutes if the sauce needs to thicken.
Remove the bay leaf, taste and adjust salt, and finish with a scattering of fresh parsley.

name: Baked Ziti
steps: Cook pasta per package directions.
Toss with ricotta and sauce, top with mozzarella.
Bake at 375F for 15-18 min.
steps_detailed: Preheat the oven to 375F. Bring a pot of salted water to a boil and cook the pasta 2 minutes short of package directions.
Mix the ricotta, egg, garlic, and dried basil together in a bowl until smooth.
Toss the drained pasta with the pasta sauce.
Layer half the sauced pasta in a baking dish, dollop half the ricotta mixture over it, then repeat with the remaining pasta and ricotta.
Top with mozzarella and parmesan.
Bake uncovered 18-20 minutes until bubbling and golden. Rest 5 minutes, then garnish with fresh basil.

name: Italian Sausage and Peppers

ingredients.note: sliced, or Italian sausage if available

steps: Saute sausage, pepper, and onion together 10-12 min.
Serve on bread.

ingredients_full.note: sliced, or Italian sausage if available
slice, or a roll if available

steps_detailed: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the sausage slices and cook 3-4 minutes until browned on both sides. Remove to a plate.
Add the peppers and onion to the same pan, letting the rendered fat coat them. Cook 8-10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until softened and starting to char at the edges.
Add the garlic, oregano, and red pepper flakes, cooking 30 seconds until fragrant.
Return the sausage to the pan and toss everything together, cooking another 2 minutes to heat through.
Finish with a drizzle of balsamic vinegar off heat — it adds a bright note that cuts through the richness. Serve over or alongside the bread.

name: Caprese Chicken Jar
steps: Layer chicken, tomatoes, mozzarella, and basil in a jar.
steps_detailed: Whisk the balsamic vinegar and olive oil together.
Layer the tomatoes, mozzarella, and basil in the bottom of a jar, drizzling with a little of the dressing as you go.
Top with the sliced chicken and remaining dressing before sealing.

name: Italian Tuna Pasta Salad
steps: Toss all ingredients together with dressing.
steps_detailed: Combine the pasta, tuna, tomatoes, red onion, olives, and capers in a bowl.
Toss with the Italian dressing until evenly coated.
Finish with fresh parsley. This keeps well chilled for 2-3 days — the flavor actually improves after a few hours.

name: Italian White Bean Salad
steps: Toss all ingredients together with oil.
steps_detailed: Whisk the olive oil, vinegar, and garlic together for the dressing.
Combine the beans, tomatoes, onion, and basil in a bowl. Toss with the dressing.
Let sit 10 minutes if you have time — this softens the raw onion bite and lets the flavors meld. Top with parmesan before serving.

name: Italian Baked Frittata Cups
steps: Whisk eggs with pepper and parmesan.
Bake in a greased muffin cup at 375F for 15-18 min.
steps_detailed: Preheat the oven to 375F and grease a muffin tin.
Heat olive oil in a small skillet over medium heat. Saute the onion and bell pepper 4-5 minutes until softened.
Whisk the eggs with the milk, salt, and pepper.
Divide the sauteed vegetables among the muffin cups, then pour the egg mixture over, filling each about two-thirds full (they'll puff up).
Sprinkle parmesan over the tops.
Bake 15-18 minutes until puffed and set in the center. Let cool 2 minutes before removing — they'll deflate slightly, which is normal. Garnish with fresh basil.

name: Roasted Garlic White Bean Dip
steps: Blend beans, garlic, and oil with a splash of water until smooth.
steps_detailed: If roasting the garlic fresh, wrap unpeeled cloves in foil with a drizzle of oil and roast at 400F for 25-30 minutes until soft and caramelized, then squeeze out the cloves.
Combine the beans, roasted garlic, olive oil, and lemon juice in a food processor or blender.
Blend until smooth, adding a splash of water if it's too thick.
Stir in the rosemary, or blend it in for a smoother finish. Season with salt to taste.

name: Parmesan Roasted Chickpeas
steps: Toss chickpeas with oil and parmesan.
Roast at 400F for 20-25 min until crisp.
steps_detailed: Preheat the oven to 400F. Pat the chickpeas thoroughly dry with a towel — moisture is the enemy of crispiness here.
Toss with olive oil, garlic, and oregano.
Spread in a single layer on a baking sheet and roast 20-25 minutes, shaking the pan halfway, until deeply golden and crunchy.
Toss immediately with the parmesan while still hot, so it melts slightly and clings to the chickpeas.

name: Tiramisu Yogurt Cup
steps: Swirl cocoa and honey into yogurt.

ingredients_full.note: tbsp, softened, as a mascarpone stand-in
tsp, dissolved in 1 tsp hot water

steps_detailed: Whisk the cream cheese until smooth, then beat in the yogurt, honey, and vanilla until fully combined.
Stir the dissolved espresso through — just enough to give a coffee note without watering down the mixture.
Sift the cocoa powder into the mixture and fold through, or layer it in between spoonfuls for a marbled look.
Chill at least 20 minutes. Dust with a little extra cocoa powder just before serving.

name: Panna Cotta (Light)
steps: Warm milk with honey and vanilla.
Chill in small cups at least 3 hours (add 1 tsp gelatin if you have it, for a firmer set).
steps_detailed: Sprinkle the gelatin over 2 tablespoons of cold water in a small bowl and let it sit 5 minutes to bloom (soften) — this step matters, skipping it can leave gritty lumps in the finished panna cotta.
Warm the milk, cream, and honey in a saucepan over medium-low heat, stirring until the honey dissolves. Don't let it boil.
Remove from heat and whisk in the bloomed gelatin until fully dissolved, then stir in the vanilla.
Pour into small serving cups or ramekins and chill for at least 3-4 hours, until fully set.
Run a thin knife around the edge if unmolding onto a plate, or serve directly in the cup.

name: Marinara Sauce (Homemade)
steps: Saute garlic in oil, add tomatoes, simmer 15-20 min.
Stir in basil at the end.
steps_detailed: Heat olive oil in a saucepan over medium heat. Add the onion and cook 5-6 minutes until translucent.
Add the garlic and cook 30 seconds until fragrant, being careful not to let it brown or it turns bitter.
Stir in the tomato paste and cook 1 minute to deepen its flavor.
Add the diced tomatoes, oregano, red pepper flakes, and sugar. Bring to a simmer.
Simmer uncovered 20-25 minutes, stirring occasionally, until thickened to your liking — mash any large tomato chunks against the side of the pot as it cooks if you want a smoother sauce.
Stir in the fresh basil at the very end, off heat, so it stays bright rather than turning dark and bitter. Taste and adjust salt.

name: Italian Chopped Salad
steps: Toss all ingredients together with dressing.
steps_detailed: Combine the lettuce, tomatoes, red onion, and chickpeas in a large bowl.
Add the salami, provolone, and olives.
Toss everything with the Italian dressing right before serving so the lettuce doesn't wilt if you're making it ahead — keep the dressing separate until then.

name: Minestrone Soup
steps: Simmer tomatoes, zucchini, and broth 10 min.
Add beans and pasta, simmer 8-10 min until pasta is tender.
steps_detailed: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add the onion, carrot, and celery and cook 6-8 minutes until softened.
Add the garlic and cook 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the tomatoes, broth, oregano, and bay leaf. Bring to a boil, then reduce to a simmer.
Add the zucchini and simmer 10 minutes.
Add the pasta and beans, and cook another 8-10 minutes until the pasta is tender.
Remove the bay leaf. Taste and adjust salt. Serve topped with fresh basil and parmesan.

name: Muffuletta-Style Sandwich
steps: Layer meats, cheese, and chopped olives between bread slices.

ingredients_full.note: slices, or a round roll if available

steps_detailed: Combine both types of chopped olives with the garlic, olive oil, vinegar, and oregano — this olive salad is the signature muffuletta element, and it's better if it sits for at least 15 minutes to let the flavors meld.
Spread the olive salad generously on both slices of bread.
Layer the salami, ham, and provolone between the bread.
For the classic effect, wrap the sandwich tightly and let it sit for 10-15 minutes (or press it under a heavy pan) so the olive oil soaks slightly into the bread before serving.
